Employees.Specializations: Anomaly Detection, Log Analysis, SLO Tracking

WorkContainers.Name: Operation 'Phoenix' - Cloud Migration
WorkContainers.Description: Zero-downtime migration of the production cluster from AWS to Fly.io for performance optimization and cost reduction.

WorkItems.Description: Terraform State Migration and Provider Update

Comments.Body: State file locked and successfully migrated to the new backend. No drift detected.

WorkItems.Description: Security Audit of RBAC Policies (New Region)

Comments.Body: Found 3 orphan service accounts. Need cleanup before production rollout.
🤖 **GERDA Insights**: Potential privilege escalation path detected in 'Deployment-Bot' role. Similarity to CVE-2024-XXXX: 88%. Audit recommended for secret exposure.
ClickHouse cluster is provisioned. Replicating data from old Postgres. Estimated 2TB to migrate.

WorkContainers.Name: SaaS Platform v3.0 Release
WorkContainers.Description: Major platform release with new dashboard, real-time collaboration, and AI-powered search.

Comments.Body: WebSocket server is handling 50k concurrent connections in staging. Need to optimize for production scale.
Vector search index is built. Embedding model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) is deployed. Query latency is 120ms.

WorkItems.Description: New Dashboard UI with Dark Mode

Comments.Body: App is in TestFlight. Beta users reporting positive feedback. One crash on iOS 17 to investigate.

WorkItems.Description: E2E Test Suite for v3.0 Features

WorkContainers.Name: Security Hardening Q1 2026
WorkContainers.Description: Comprehensive security hardening initiative including SOC 2 preparation, penetration testing, and infrastructure audit.

Comments.Body: Documentation is 70% complete. Need to finalize evidence for CC6.1 and CC6.2 controls.
HashiCorp Vault is configured. Auto-rotation policy: 90 days for API keys, 30 days for DB credentials.

WorkContainers.Name: Data Platform Modernization
WorkContainers.Description: Migration from legacy ETL pipelines to modern streaming architecture with real-time analytics.

Comments.Body: Kafka cluster is live with 3 brokers. Replication factor 3, min ISR 2. Throughput: 150k msg/sec.
Dashboard is consuming Kafka streams. Charts are updating every 5 seconds. Need to add filtering capabilities.

WorkItems.Description: Data Pipeline Migration: Batch to Streaming

WorkContainers.Name: Infrastructure Cost Optimization
WorkContainers.Description: Reduce cloud infrastructure costs by 30% through right-sizing, reserved instances, and spot instance usage.

Comments.Body: Analysis complete. 25% of instances are over-provisioned. Potential savings: $12k/month.
Purchased 1-year convertible RIs for baseline compute. Additional savings: $8k/month.

WorkContainers.Name: Customer Support Portal Redesign
WorkContainers.Description: Complete redesign of the customer support portal with improved self-service, knowledge base integration, and chat.

Comments.Body: User interviews completed with 12 customers. Key insight: 80% want better search in KB.
Our design team is unable to upload assets. The API is timing out constantly. This is blocking our client delivery.

🤖 **GERDA Anomaly Engine**: Traffic wave detected from IP 192.x.x.x. Correlated with database lock in 'assets_table'. Autoscaling group reached max capacity. Suggesting immediate cache warmup for region 'ams'.
All database connections are exhausted. Application is returning 500 errors. Need immediate intervention.
🤖 **GERDA Crisis Autopilot**: Connection pool saturation detected. 250/250 connections active. 47 queries waiting >30s. Auto-recommendation: restart connection pool and investigate connection leaks.
auth-service pods are in CrashLoopBackOff. Users cannot log in. This is affecting all customers.
🤖 **GERDA Analysis**: CrashLoop pattern: OOMKilled. Memory limit 256MB insufficient after recent JWT library update. Recommendation: increase memory limit to 512MB.

Comments.Body: Customer requested full data export under GDPR Article 20. Need all personal data in machine-readable format (JSON).

UnassignedWorkItems.Description: Mobile app crashes on Android 14 - reported by multiple users

Comments.Body: Our customers are reporting app crashes on Android 14 devices. The crash happens when opening the checkout page.
Our auditors need access to your SOC 2 Type II report for our vendor assessment. Can you share it under NDA?

UnassignedWorkItems.Description: Feature Request: Role-based access control for team invites

Comments.Body: We need the ability to assign roles when inviting team members (Admin, Editor, Viewer). Currently everyone gets Admin.

UnassignedWorkItems.Description: Email delivery failure - SendGrid reputation drop

Comments.Body: Our transactional emails are bouncing. SendGrid reputation score dropped to 78. Need to investigate bounce rate spike.
We are hitting the 1000 req/min rate limit during our peak hours. Need increase to 5000 req/min for Enterprise tier.

UnassignedWorkItems.Description: Memory leak in background job processor

Comments.Body: Background job processor memory usage grows 200MB/hour. After 12 hours it OOMs and restarts. Need heap dump analysis.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Fly.io Deployment Best Practices
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
**Core Principles:**
1. Multi-region deployments are mandatory for tier-1 apps.
2. Use WireGuard for secure cross-region DB access.
3. Keep Docker images < 200MB.

**Autoscale Config:**
Add `[http_service.concurrency]` to fly.toml with `soft_limit = 20`.

**Monitoring:**
* Use Fly's built-in metrics.
* Set up external alerting with Prometheus.
* Health checks: TCP + HTTP endpoint.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: RBAC Troubleshooting Guide
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
How to debug 'Access Denied' errors in the new portal.

**Steps:**
* Check user's `OrganizationId` claim.
* Verify `TenantAdmin` flag in the JWT.
* Run `just verify-permissions <Email>` in the CLI.

**Common Issues:**
1. Orphan service accounts with old roles.
2. Missing `ResourceAction` claims.
3. Caching delay in permission resolution.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Kubernetes Debugging Playbook
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Common Kubernetes issues and how to debug them.

**Pod CrashLoopBackOff:**
1. `kubectl logs <pod>` - check application logs.
2. `kubectl describe pod <pod>` - check events.
3. `kubectl get events` - cluster-wide events.
4. Check resource limits (OOMKilled).

**Service Unavailable:**
1. Verify endpoints: `kubectl get endpoints`.
2. Check ingress rules.
3. Verify DNS resolution.

**High Memory Usage:**
1. Profile application memory.
2. Check for goroutine leaks.
3. Review cache sizes.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: ClickHouse Performance Tuning
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Performance optimization for ClickHouse analytics.

**Query Optimization:**
* Use `PREWHERE` instead of `WHERE` for large tables.
* Avoid `SELECT *` - specify columns.
* Use `LIMIT` with `ORDER BY`.

**Schema Design:**
* Use `MergeTree` engine for time-series.
* Choose partition key carefully (usually date).
* Use `ORDER BY` for primary key.

**Hardware:**
* SSD is mandatory for hot data.
* RAM should be 2x dataset size for caching.
* CPU: more cores > faster cores.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: React Performance Best Practices
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Performance optimization for React applications.

**Rendering:**
* Use `React.memo` for pure components.
* Use `useMemo` for expensive computations.
* Use `useCallback` for event handlers.

**State Management:**
* Keep state close to where it's used.
* Avoid prop drilling.
* Use context sparingly.

**Bundle Size:**
* Tree-shake unused code.
* Code-split routes.
* Use dynamic imports.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: SOC 2 Compliance Checklist
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Checklist for SOC 2 Type II compliance preparation.

**CC6.1 - Logical Access Security:**
* MFA enabled for all users.
* Role-based access control.
* Regular access reviews.

**CC6.2 - System Operations:**
* Change management process.
* System monitoring and alerting.
* Incident response procedures.

**CC7.1 - System Monitoring:**
* Log collection and retention.
* Audit trail completeness.
* Anomaly detection.

**CC7.2 - Incident Response:**
* Documented IR plan.
* Escalation procedures.
* Post-incident reviews.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: API Design Guidelines
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
REST API design guidelines for Liberty Systems.

**URL Structure:**
* Use nouns, not verbs: `/users` not `/getUsers`.
* Use plural: `/users` not `/user`.
* Nest resources: `/users/123/orders`.

**HTTP Methods:**
* GET - read
* POST - create
* PUT - full update
* PATCH - partial update
* DELETE - remove

**Response Format:**
* Always return JSON.
* Use consistent error format.
* Include pagination metadata.

**Versioning:**
* URL versioning: `/v1/users`.
* Header versioning: `Accept: application/vnd.api.v1+json`.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Testing Strategy - E2E and Unit
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Comprehensive testing strategy for the platform.

**Unit Tests:**
* Jest for JavaScript/TypeScript.
* xUnit for .NET.
* Target: 80% coverage.
* Mock external dependencies.

**Integration Tests:**
* Test API endpoints.
* Test database queries.
* Test message queues.

**E2E Tests:**
* Playwright for web.
* Maestro for mobile.
* Test critical user journeys.

**CI/CD:**
* Run unit tests on every PR.
* Run integration tests on merge.
* Run E2E tests nightly.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Terraform State Management
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Best practices for managing Terraform state.

**Remote State:**
* Always use remote state (S3, Terraform Cloud).
* Enable state locking.
* Enable versioning.

**State Security:**
* Encrypt state at rest.
* Restrict access to state files.
* Never commit state to Git.

**State Structure:**
* Use workspaces for environments.
* Use modules for reuse.
* Keep state small and focused.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Mobile App Release Process
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Process for releasing mobile apps to App Store and Play Store.

**Pre-release:**
1. Run full E2E test suite.
2. Check crash analytics (no new crashes).
3. Verify translations are complete.
4. Test on physical devices.

**Release:**
1. Create release branch.
2. Update version numbers.
3. Build signed binaries.
4. Submit to stores.
5. Monitor rollout (staged release).

**Post-release:**
1. Monitor crash rate.
2. Check app store reviews.
3. Respond to critical issues within 4 hours.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Data Pipeline Architecture
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Architecture overview of the data platform.

**Ingestion:**
* Kafka for real-time streaming.
* S3 for batch imports.
* API for event submission.

**Processing:**
* Kafka Streams for transformations.
* dbt for data modeling.
* Spark for heavy batch jobs.

**Storage:**
* ClickHouse for analytics.
* Postgres for transactional.
* S3 for raw data lake.

**Consumption:**
* Grafana for dashboards.
* API for application data.
* Export for external tools.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Incident Response Playbook
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Standard incident response procedures.

**Severity Levels:**
* SEV1: Complete service outage.
* SEV2: Major functionality degraded.
* SEV3: Minor impact, workaround available.
* SEV4: Cosmetic or non-urgent.

**Response Process:**
1. **Detect**: Alert from monitoring.
2. **Acknowledge**: Within 5 minutes.
3. **Assess**: Determine severity and impact.
4. **Communicate**: Post in #incidents channel.
5. **Resolve**: Fix the issue.
6. **Review**: Post-mortem within 48 hours.

**Escalation:**
* SEV1: Page on-call immediately.
* SEV2: Notify team lead within 30 min.
* SEV3: Handle during business hours.
